paragraphs.text:
%pyspark

import time

# Force Spark to start the SparkContext if it hasn't already
print("App ID:", spark.sparkContext.applicationId)
print("Master:", spark.sparkContext.master)

# Wait up to 60 seconds for executors to register with the driver
target_executors = 4  # we want at least this many (excluding driver)
max_wait = 60
poll_interval = 5

print(f"\nWaiting up to {max_wait}s for executors to register...")
print(f"Target: at least {target_executors} executors\n")

elapsed = 0
while elapsed < max_wait:
    # getExecutorMemoryStatus includes driver, so subtract 1 to get true executor count
    total_count = spark.sparkContext._jsc.sc().getExecutorMemoryStatus().size()
    executor_count = total_count - 1
    parallelism = spark.sparkContext.defaultParallelism
    
    print(f"  t={elapsed:>2}s  executors={executor_count}  parallelism={parallelism}")
    
    if executor_count >= target_executors:
        print(f"\n✓ Got {executor_count} executors. Proceeding.")
        break
    
    time.sleep(poll_interval)
    elapsed += poll_interval
else:
    print(f"\n⚠ Only got {executor_count} executors after {max_wait}s.")
    print("  Cluster may be saturated. Check YARN UI to see other users' allocations.")
    print("  Options: wait, reduce executor request, or sample the training data.")

Target: at least 4 executors

paragraphs.text:
%pyspark
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, length, when, lit, rand
from pyspark.ml import Pipeline
from pyspark.ml.feature import Tokenizer, RegexTokenizer, StopWordsRemover, HashingTF, IDF, CountVectorizer
from pyspark.ml.classification import LogisticRegression
from pyspark.ml.evaluation import BinaryClassificationEvaluator, MulticlassClassificationEvaluator
import time

# Load the parsed parquet, cast types properly
df_full = (spark.read.parquet("hdfs:///user/sic825/steam_reviews/parsed_full.parquet")
                .withColumn("recommended", (col("recommended") == "True").cast("boolean"))
                .withColumn("votes_helpful", col("votes_helpful").cast("long"))
                .withColumn("playtime_forever", col("playtime_forever").cast("double"))
                .withColumn("steam_purchase", (col("steam_purchase") == "True").cast("boolean")))

# Filter to English only
df_en = df_full.filter(col("language") == "english")

# Drop rows with missing critical fields
df_en = df_en.filter(col("review").isNotNull() & col("recommended").isNotNull())

# Clip review length to handle outliers (5000 chars is plenty)
df_en = df_en.filter((length(col("review")) >= 5) & (length(col("review")) <= 5000))

df_en.cache()
n_en = df_en.count()
print(f"English reviews after filtering: {n_en:,}")

df_en.printSchema()
df_en.select("review", "recommended").show(3, truncate=80)

paragraphs.text:
%pyspark

# Stratified split using sampleBy: 70% train, 15% val, 15% test
# We split each class (True/False) separately to preserve the class ratio

# Add a random column for splitting
df_en = df_en.withColumn("rand_split", rand(seed=42))

train_en = df_en.filter(col("rand_split") < 0.70).drop("rand_split")
val_en = df_en.filter((col("rand_split") >= 0.70) & (col("rand_split") < 0.85)).drop("rand_split")
test_en = df_en.filter(col("rand_split") >= 0.85).drop("rand_split")

# Cache the splits — we'll hit them many times
train_en.cache()
val_en.cache()
test_en.cache()

# Verify sizes and class balance
print(f"Train: {train_en.count():,}")
print(f"Val:   {val_en.count():,}")
print(f"Test:  {test_en.count():,}")

print("\nTrain class balance:")
train_en.groupBy("recommended").count().show()

print("Val class balance:")
val_en.groupBy("recommended").count().show()

%pyspark

# Compute class weights inversely proportional to class frequency
# Negative class is ~11%, so it gets ~9x weight; positive is ~89%, gets ~1.1x weight
# This tells the LR optimizer to care more about getting negatives right

n_train = train_en.count()
n_pos = train_en.filter(col("recommended") == True).count()
n_neg = n_train - n_pos

# Inverse frequency weighting
w_pos = n_train / (2.0 * n_pos)
w_neg = n_train / (2.0 * n_neg)

print(f"Positive weight: {w_pos:.4f}")
print(f"Negative weight: {w_neg:.4f}")

# Add weight column to train
train_en_w = train_en.withColumn(
    "class_weight",
    when(col("recommended") == True, lit(w_pos)).otherwise(lit(w_neg))
)
# Also need an integer label for LR
train_en_w = train_en_w.withColumn("label", col("recommended").cast("double"))
val_en_w = val_en.withColumn("label", col("recommended").cast("double"))
test_en_w = test_en.withColumn("label", col("recommended").cast("double"))

train_en_w.cache()
val_en_w.cache()
test_en_w.cache()

print(f"\nTrain rows: {train_en_w.count():,}")
train_en_w.select("review", "label", "class_weight").show(3, truncate=60)

%pyspark

# RegexTokenizer splits on non-word characters and lowercases — better than basic Tokenizer
tokenizer = RegexTokenizer(
    inputCol="review",
    outputCol="tokens",
    pattern="\\W+",
    toLowercase=True,
    minTokenLength=2  # drop single chars
)

# Remove English stop words
stopword_remover = StopWordsRemover(
    inputCol="tokens",
    outputCol="tokens_clean"
)

# HashingTF for term frequency — using a fixed feature size for memory efficiency
# 2^18 = 262144 is a reasonable hash space for English NLP
hashing_tf = HashingTF(
    inputCol="tokens_clean",
    outputCol="raw_features",
    numFeatures=262144
)

# IDF to downweight common terms
idf = IDF(
    inputCol="raw_features",
    outputCol="features",
    minDocFreq=5  # ignore terms appearing in fewer than 5 docs
)

# Logistic regression with class weighting
lr = LogisticRegression(
    featuresCol="features",
    labelCol="label",
    weightCol="class_weight",
    maxIter=20,
    regParam=0.01,
    elasticNetParam=0.0  # pure L2
)

pipeline = Pipeline(stages=[tokenizer, stopword_remover, hashing_tf, idf, lr])

print("Pipeline built. Stages:")
for i, stage in enumerate(pipeline.getStages()):
    print(f"  {i}: {type(stage).__name__}")

msg.data:
Pipeline built. Stages:
  0: RegexTokenizer
  1: StopWordsRemover
  2: HashingTF
  3: IDF
  4: LogisticRegression

paragraphs.text:
%pyspark

start = time.time()

print("Fitting pipeline on training data...")
model = pipeline.fit(train_en_w)

elapsed = time.time() - start
print(f"Done in {elapsed:.1f}s")

# Inspect the trained LR model
lr_model = model.stages[-1]
print(f"\nLR coefficients: {lr_model.coefficients.size:,} features")
print(f"LR intercept: {lr_model.intercept:.4f}")
print(f"Total iterations: {lr_model.summary.totalIterations}")

Total iterations: 21

paragraphs.text:
%pyspark

# Generate predictions on the validation set
preds_val = model.transform(val_en_w)
preds_val.cache()

# Show a few predictions
preds_val.select("review", "label", "prediction", "probability").show(5, truncate=60)

# Binary classification metrics (uses the probability column)
auc_eval = BinaryClassificationEvaluator(
    labelCol="label",
    rawPredictionCol="rawPrediction",
    metricName="areaUnderROC"
)
pr_eval = BinaryClassificationEvaluator(
    labelCol="label",
    rawPredictionCol="rawPrediction",
    metricName="areaUnderPR"
)

auc = auc_eval.evaluate(preds_val)
pr_auc = pr_eval.evaluate(preds_val)

print(f"\nVal ROC AUC:  {auc:.4f}")
print(f"Val PR AUC:   {pr_auc:.4f}")

# Multiclass metrics for accuracy / F1 (uses the prediction column)
acc_eval = MulticlassClassificationEvaluator(labelCol="label", predictionCol="prediction", metricName="accuracy")
f1_eval = MulticlassClassificationEvaluator(labelCol="label", predictionCol="prediction", metricName="f1")
prec_eval = MulticlassClassificationEvaluator(labelCol="label", predictionCol="prediction", metricName="weightedPrecision")
recall_eval = MulticlassClassificationEvaluator(labelCol="label", predictionCol="prediction", metricName="weightedRecall")

print(f"Val Accuracy:           {acc_eval.evaluate(preds_val):.4f}")
print(f"Val Weighted F1:        {f1_eval.evaluate(preds_val):.4f}")
print(f"Val Weighted Precision: {prec_eval.evaluate(preds_val):.4f}")
print(f"Val Weighted Recall:    {recall_eval.evaluate(preds_val):.4f}")

%pyspark

# Confusion matrix
cm = (preds_val
        .groupBy("label", "prediction")
        .count()
        .orderBy("label", "prediction"))

print("Confusion matrix (label, prediction, count):")
cm.show()

# Compute per-class precision, recall, F1 manually
# label 0.0 = False (negative), label 1.0 = True (positive)
tp_pos = preds_val.filter((col("label") == 1.0) & (col("prediction") == 1.0)).count()
fp_pos = preds_val.filter((col("label") == 0.0) & (col("prediction") == 1.0)).count()
fn_pos = preds_val.filter((col("label") == 1.0) & (col("prediction") == 0.0)).count()
tn_pos = preds_val.filter((col("label") == 0.0) & (col("prediction") == 0.0)).count()

# Negative class metrics (the one that matters most for imbalanced)
precision_neg = tn_pos / (tn_pos + fn_pos) if (tn_pos + fn_pos) > 0 else 0
recall_neg = tn_pos / (tn_pos + fp_pos) if (tn_pos + fp_pos) > 0 else 0
f1_neg = 2 * precision_neg * recall_neg / (precision_neg + recall_neg) if (precision_neg + recall_neg) > 0 else 0

# Positive class metrics
precision_pos = tp_pos / (tp_pos + fp_pos) if (tp_pos + fp_pos) > 0 else 0
recall_pos = tp_pos / (tp_pos + fn_pos) if (tp_pos + fn_pos) > 0 else 0
f1_pos = 2 * precision_pos * recall_pos / (precision_pos + recall_pos) if (precision_pos + recall_pos) > 0 else 0

print(f"\nNegative class (not recommended):")
print(f"  Precision: {precision_neg:.4f}")
print(f"  Recall:    {recall_neg:.4f}")
print(f"  F1:        {f1_neg:.4f}")

print(f"\nPositive class (recommended):")
print(f"  Precision: {precision_pos:.4f}")
print(f"  Recall:    {recall_pos:.4f}")
print(f"  F1:        {f1_pos:.4f}")

# Macro F1 = unweighted average of per-class F1
macro_f1 = (f1_neg + f1_pos) / 2
print(f"\nMacro F1: {macro_f1:.4f}")

paragraphs.text:
%pyspark

preds_test = model.transform(test_en_w)
preds_test.cache()

# Same metrics as before
auc_test = auc_eval.evaluate(preds_test)
pr_auc_test = pr_eval.evaluate(preds_test)

tp = preds_test.filter((col("label") == 1.0) & (col("prediction") == 1.0)).count()
fp = preds_test.filter((col("label") == 0.0) & (col("prediction") == 1.0)).count()
fn = preds_test.filter((col("label") == 1.0) & (col("prediction") == 0.0)).count()
tn = preds_test.filter((col("label") == 0.0) & (col("prediction") == 0.0)).count()

prec_neg_t = tn / (tn + fn) if (tn + fn) > 0 else 0
rec_neg_t = tn / (tn + fp) if (tn + fp) > 0 else 0
f1_neg_t = 2 * prec_neg_t * rec_neg_t / (prec_neg_t + rec_neg_t) if (prec_neg_t + rec_neg_t) > 0 else 0

prec_pos_t = tp / (tp + fp) if (tp + fp) > 0 else 0
rec_pos_t = tp / (tp + fn) if (tp + fn) > 0 else 0
f1_pos_t = 2 * prec_pos_t * rec_pos_t / (prec_pos_t + rec_pos_t) if (prec_pos_t + rec_pos_t) > 0 else 0

print("===== TEST SET RESULTS — English =====")
print(f"ROC AUC:      {auc_test:.4f}")
print(f"PR AUC:       {pr_auc_test:.4f}")
print(f"\nConfusion matrix:")
print(f"  TN={tn:,}  FP={fp:,}")
print(f"  FN={fn:,}  TP={tp:,}")
print(f"\nNegative class: P={prec_neg_t:.4f}  R={rec_neg_t:.4f}  F1={f1_neg_t:.4f}")
print(f"Positive class: P={prec_pos_t:.4f}  R={rec_pos_t:.4f}  F1={f1_pos_t:.4f}")
print(f"Macro F1: {(f1_neg_t + f1_pos_t)/2:.4f}")

# Save model to HDFS for the report / reproducibility
model.write().overwrite().save("hdfs:///user/sic825/steam_reviews/models/english_lr_v1")
print("\nModel saved to HDFS.")

paragraphs.text:
%pyspark
from pyspark.sql.functions import udf
from pyspark.sql.types import FloatType

# Extract the probability of the positive class from the probability vector
prob_pos_udf = udf(lambda v: float(v[1]), FloatType())
preds_test_with_prob = preds_test.withColumn("prob_pos", prob_pos_udf(col("probability")))
preds_test_with_prob.cache()

# False positives — most confident "positive" that were actually negative
print("=" * 80)
print("Top 15 confident FALSE POSITIVES (predicted recommend, actually NOT recommend)")
print("=" * 80)
(preds_test_with_prob
  .filter((col("label") == 0.0) & (col("prediction") == 1.0))
  .orderBy(col("prob_pos").desc())
  .select("review", "prob_pos")
  .show(15, truncate=140))

# False negatives — most confident "negative" that were actually positive
print("=" * 80)
print("Top 15 confident FALSE NEGATIVES (predicted NOT recommend, actually recommend)")
print("=" * 80)
(preds_test_with_prob
  .filter((col("label") == 1.0) & (col("prediction") == 0.0))
  .orderBy(col("prob_pos").asc())
  .select("review", "prob_pos")
  .show(15, truncate=140))

# Borderline cases — predictions hovering around 0.5
print("=" * 80)
print("Top 10 BORDERLINE predictions (probability between 0.45 and 0.55)")
print("=" * 80)
(preds_test_with_prob
  .filter((col("prob_pos") >= 0.45) & (col("prob_pos") <= 0.55))
  .select("review", "label", "prediction", "prob_pos")
  .show(10, truncate=120))

paragraphs.text:
%pyspark
from pyspark.ml.feature import CountVectorizer
from pyspark.ml import Pipeline
import time

# Replace HashingTF with CountVectorizer to get a vocabulary
tokenizer_cv = RegexTokenizer(
    inputCol="review",
    outputCol="tokens",
    pattern="\\W+",
    toLowercase=True,
    minTokenLength=2
)
stopword_remover_cv = StopWordsRemover(
    inputCol="tokens",
    outputCol="tokens_clean"
)
count_vec = CountVectorizer(
    inputCol="tokens_clean",
    outputCol="raw_features",
    vocabSize=50000,    # cap vocabulary size
    minDF=10            # ignore terms in fewer than 10 docs
)
idf_cv = IDF(inputCol="raw_features", outputCol="features", minDocFreq=5)
lr_cv = LogisticRegression(
    featuresCol="features",
    labelCol="label",
    weightCol="class_weight",
    maxIter=20,
    regParam=0.01,
    elasticNetParam=0.0
)

pipeline_cv = Pipeline(stages=[tokenizer_cv, stopword_remover_cv, count_vec, idf_cv, lr_cv])

start = time.time()
print("Fitting CountVectorizer pipeline...")
model_cv = pipeline_cv.fit(train_en_w)
elapsed = time.time() - start
print(f"Done in {elapsed:.1f}s")

# Evaluate on validation
preds_val_cv = model_cv.transform(val_en_w)
auc_cv = auc_eval.evaluate(preds_val_cv)
print(f"Val ROC AUC (CountVectorizer): {auc_cv:.4f}")

import numpy as np

# Extract the vocabulary from the CountVectorizer model and coefficients from LR
cv_model = model_cv.stages[2]   # CountVectorizer is stage 2
lr_cv_model = model_cv.stages[-1]
vocab = cv_model.vocabulary
coefs = lr_cv_model.coefficients.toArray()

# Top features predicting POSITIVE (highest positive coefficients)
top_pos_idx = np.argsort(coefs)[-30:][::-1]
print("=" * 60)
print("Top 30 words predicting POSITIVE (recommended)")
print("=" * 60)
for i in top_pos_idx:
    print(f"  {vocab[i]:<30s}  coef={coefs[i]:+.4f}")

print()

# Top features predicting NEGATIVE (most negative coefficients)
top_neg_idx = np.argsort(coefs)[:30]
print("=" * 60)
print("Top 30 words predicting NEGATIVE (not recommended)")
print("=" * 60)
for i in top_neg_idx:
    print(f"  {vocab[i]:<30s}  coef={coefs[i]:+.4f}")

%pyspark
from pyspark.ml.feature import NGram, VectorAssembler
from pyspark.ml import Pipeline
import time

tokenizer_ng = RegexTokenizer(
    inputCol="review",
    outputCol="tokens",
    pattern="\\W+",
    toLowercase=True,
    minTokenLength=2
)
stopword_remover_ng = StopWordsRemover(
    inputCol="tokens",
    outputCol="tokens_clean"
)
ngram = NGram(n=2, inputCol="tokens_clean", outputCol="bigrams")

unigram_tf = HashingTF(inputCol="tokens_clean", outputCol="unigram_features", numFeatures=131072)
bigram_tf  = HashingTF(inputCol="bigrams",      outputCol="bigram_features",  numFeatures=131072)

assembler = VectorAssembler(
    inputCols=["unigram_features", "bigram_features"],
    outputCol="raw_features"
)

idf_ng = IDF(inputCol="raw_features", outputCol="features", minDocFreq=5)
lr_ng = LogisticRegression(
    featuresCol="features",
    labelCol="label",
    weightCol="class_weight",
    maxIter=20,
    regParam=0.01,
    elasticNetParam=0.0
)

pipeline_ng = Pipeline(stages=[
    tokenizer_ng, stopword_remover_ng, ngram,
    unigram_tf, bigram_tf, assembler,
    idf_ng, lr_ng
])

start = time.time()
print("Fitting unigram + bigram pipeline...")
model_ng = pipeline_ng.fit(train_en_w)
elapsed = time.time() - start
print(f"Done in {elapsed:.1f}s")

# Evaluate on validation
preds_val_ng = model_ng.transform(val_en_w)
preds_val_ng.cache()

auc_ng = auc_eval.evaluate(preds_val_ng)
pr_auc_ng = pr_eval.evaluate(preds_val_ng)

# Per-class metrics
tp_ng = preds_val_ng.filter((col("label") == 1.0) & (col("prediction") == 1.0)).count()
fp_ng = preds_val_ng.filter((col("label") == 0.0) & (col("prediction") == 1.0)).count()
fn_ng = preds_val_ng.filter((col("label") == 1.0) & (col("prediction") == 0.0)).count()
tn_ng = preds_val_ng.filter((col("label") == 0.0) & (col("prediction") == 0.0)).count()

p_neg_ng = tn_ng / (tn_ng + fn_ng) if (tn_ng + fn_ng) > 0 else 0
r_neg_ng = tn_ng / (tn_ng + fp_ng) if (tn_ng + fp_ng) > 0 else 0
f1_neg_ng = 2 * p_neg_ng * r_neg_ng / (p_neg_ng + r_neg_ng) if (p_neg_ng + r_neg_ng) > 0 else 0

p_pos_ng = tp_ng / (tp_ng + fp_ng) if (tp_ng + fp_ng) > 0 else 0
r_pos_ng = tp_ng / (tp_ng + fn_ng) if (tp_ng + fn_ng) > 0 else 0
f1_pos_ng = 2 * p_pos_ng * r_pos_ng / (p_pos_ng + r_pos_ng) if (p_pos_ng + r_pos_ng) > 0 else 0

print(f"\n===== Bigram model (validation) =====")
print(f"ROC AUC:        {auc_ng:.4f}  (vs unigram baseline 0.9207)")
print(f"PR AUC:         {pr_auc_ng:.4f}")
print(f"Negative class: P={p_neg_ng:.4f}  R={r_neg_ng:.4f}  F1={f1_neg_ng:.4f}")
print(f"Positive class: P={p_pos_ng:.4f}  R={r_pos_ng:.4f}  F1={f1_pos_ng:.4f}")
print(f"Macro F1:       {(f1_neg_ng + f1_pos_ng) / 2:.4f}  (vs unigram baseline 0.7929)")

%pyspark
from pyspark.ml import Pipeline
import time

tokenizer_nw = RegexTokenizer(
    inputCol="review",
    outputCol="tokens",
    pattern="\\W+",
    toLowercase=True,
    minTokenLength=2
)
stopword_remover_nw = StopWordsRemover(
    inputCol="tokens",
    outputCol="tokens_clean"
)
hashing_tf_nw = HashingTF(
    inputCol="tokens_clean",
    outputCol="raw_features",
    numFeatures=262144
)
idf_nw = IDF(inputCol="raw_features", outputCol="features", minDocFreq=5)

# No weightCol — train without class weighting
lr_nw = LogisticRegression(
    featuresCol="features",
    labelCol="label",
    maxIter=20,
    regParam=0.01,
    elasticNetParam=0.0
)

pipeline_nw = Pipeline(stages=[tokenizer_nw, stopword_remover_nw, hashing_tf_nw, idf_nw, lr_nw])

start = time.time()
print("Fitting unweighted pipeline...")
model_nw = pipeline_nw.fit(train_en_w)
elapsed = time.time() - start
print(f"Done in {elapsed:.1f}s")

preds_val_nw = model_nw.transform(val_en_w)
preds_val_nw.cache()

auc_nw = auc_eval.evaluate(preds_val_nw)

tp_nw = preds_val_nw.filter((col("label") == 1.0) & (col("prediction") == 1.0)).count()
fp_nw = preds_val_nw.filter((col("label") == 0.0) & (col("prediction") == 1.0)).count()
fn_nw = preds_val_nw.filter((col("label") == 1.0) & (col("prediction") == 0.0)).count()
tn_nw = preds_val_nw.filter((col("label") == 0.0) & (col("prediction") == 0.0)).count()

p_neg_nw = tn_nw / (tn_nw + fn_nw) if (tn_nw + fn_nw) > 0 else 0
r_neg_nw = tn_nw / (tn_nw + fp_nw) if (tn_nw + fp_nw) > 0 else 0
f1_neg_nw = 2 * p_neg_nw * r_neg_nw / (p_neg_nw + r_neg_nw) if (p_neg_nw + r_neg_nw) > 0 else 0

p_pos_nw = tp_nw / (tp_nw + fp_nw) if (tp_nw + fp_nw) > 0 else 0
r_pos_nw = tp_nw / (tp_nw + fn_nw) if (tp_nw + fn_nw) > 0 else 0
f1_pos_nw = 2 * p_pos_nw * r_pos_nw / (p_pos_nw + r_pos_nw) if (p_pos_nw + r_pos_nw) > 0 else 0

print(f"\n===== UNWEIGHTED model (validation) =====")
print(f"ROC AUC:        {auc_nw:.4f}")
print(f"Confusion matrix:")
print(f"  TN={tn_nw:,}  FP={fp_nw:,}")
print(f"  FN={fn_nw:,}  TP={tp_nw:,}")
print(f"Negative class: P={p_neg_nw:.4f}  R={r_neg_nw:.4f}  F1={f1_neg_nw:.4f}")
print(f"Positive class: P={p_pos_nw:.4f}  R={r_pos_nw:.4f}  F1={f1_pos_nw:.4f}")
print(f"Macro F1:       {(f1_neg_nw + f1_pos_nw) / 2:.4f}")

print(f"\n===== Comparison: Weighted vs Unweighted =====")
print(f"{'Metric':<25} {'Weighted':<12} {'Unweighted':<12}")
print(f"{'ROC AUC':<25} {0.9207:<12.4f} {auc_nw:<12.4f}")
print(f"{'Negative class recall':<25} {0.8022:<12.4f} {r_neg_nw:<12.4f}")
print(f"{'Negative class precision':<25} {0.5384:<12.4f} {p_neg_nw:<12.4f}")
print(f"{'Negative class F1':<25} {0.6444:<12.4f} {f1_neg_nw:<12.4f}")
print(f"{'Macro F1':<25} {0.7929:<12.4f} {(f1_neg_nw + f1_pos_nw) / 2:<12.4f}")

%pyspark
import numpy as np

cv_model = model_cv.stages[2]
lr_cv_model = model_cv.stages[-1]
vocab = cv_model.vocabulary
coefs = lr_cv_model.coefficients.toArray()

# Get document frequency for each vocabulary term
# We compute this by counting non-zero entries per column in raw_features
from pyspark.ml.linalg import SparseVector, DenseVector

# Get DF from training data — vectorize the docs and count nonzeros per token
train_vectorized = model_cv.stages[2].transform(
    model_cv.stages[1].transform(
        model_cv.stages[0].transform(train_en_w)))

# Aggregate document frequencies across all rows
def vec_to_indices(v):
    return v.indices.tolist() if hasattr(v, 'indices') else []

from pyspark.sql.functions import explode, array
indices_udf = udf(lambda v: v.indices.tolist() if hasattr(v, 'indices') else [], "array<int>")

df_counts = (train_vectorized
              .select(explode(indices_udf("raw_features")).alias("idx"))
              .groupBy("idx")
              .count()
              .collect())

doc_freq = {row['idx']: row['count'] for row in df_counts}

# Now filter top features by frequency threshold
MIN_DOC_FREQ = 1000

# Top positive
ranked_pos = sorted([(i, coefs[i], doc_freq.get(i, 0)) for i in range(len(coefs))],
                    key=lambda x: -x[1])
filtered_pos = [(i, c, df) for i, c, df in ranked_pos if df >= MIN_DOC_FREQ][:30]

print(f"Top 30 words predicting POSITIVE (with doc_freq >= {MIN_DOC_FREQ})")
print("=" * 70)
for i, c, df in filtered_pos:
    print(f"  {vocab[i]:<25s}  coef={c:+.4f}  doc_freq={df:,}")

# Top negative
ranked_neg = sorted([(i, coefs[i], doc_freq.get(i, 0)) for i in range(len(coefs))],
                    key=lambda x: x[1])
filtered_neg = [(i, c, df) for i, c, df in ranked_neg if df >= MIN_DOC_FREQ][:30]

print(f"\nTop 30 words predicting NEGATIVE (with doc_freq >= {MIN_DOC_FREQ})")
print("=" * 70)
for i, c, df in filtered_neg:
    print(f"  {vocab[i]:<25s}  coef={c:+.4f}  doc_freq={df:,}")

paragraphs.text:
%pyspark
# Word2Vec sample setup
from pyspark.ml.feature import Word2Vec, RegexTokenizer, StopWordsRemover

# Tokenize the English text using the same tokenizer as our other English models
# Word2Vec needs token arrays — same approach as the bigram pipeline
tokenizer_w2v_en = RegexTokenizer(
    inputCol="review",
    outputCol="tokens_raw",
    pattern="\\W+",
    toLowercase=True,
    minTokenLength=2
)
stopword_remover_w2v_en = StopWordsRemover(
    inputCol="tokens_raw",
    outputCol="tokens"
)

# Fit and apply tokenizers to the train/val sets so they have a "tokens" column
# Note: train_en_w and val_en_w from the earlier paragraphs already exist with class_weight and label
from pyspark.ml import Pipeline

prep_pipeline = Pipeline(stages=[tokenizer_w2v_en, stopword_remover_w2v_en])
prep_model = prep_pipeline.fit(train_en_w)

train_en_tok = prep_model.transform(train_en_w).cache()
val_en_tok = prep_model.transform(val_en_w).cache()

# Sample 30% to match Schinese setup
train_en_w2v_sample = train_en_tok.sample(fraction=0.30, seed=42).cache()

print(f"Full English training set: {train_en_tok.count():,}")
print(f"30% sample for Word2Vec: {train_en_w2v_sample.count():,}")
print("\nSample tokenized row:")
train_en_w2v_sample.select("tokens", "label", "class_weight").show(3, truncate=80)

%pyspark
# Word2Vec training

from pyspark.ml.feature import Word2Vec
from pyspark.ml.classification import LogisticRegression
from pyspark.ml import Pipeline
import time

# Same hyperparameters as Schinese for fair cross-language comparison
w2v_en = Word2Vec(
    vectorSize=50,
    minCount=10,
    inputCol="tokens",
    outputCol="features",
    maxIter=3,
    windowSize=5,
    seed=42
)

lr_w2v_en = LogisticRegression(
    featuresCol="features",
    labelCol="label",
    weightCol="class_weight",
    maxIter=10,
    regParam=0.01,
    elasticNetParam=0.0
)

pipeline_w2v_en = Pipeline(stages=[w2v_en, lr_w2v_en])

start = time.time()
print("Fitting English Word2Vec pipeline (sampled, 50-dim, 3 epochs)...")

model_w2v_en = pipeline_w2v_en.fit(train_en_w2v_sample)

elapsed = time.time() - start
print(f"Done in {elapsed:.1f}s")

w2v_en_model = model_w2v_en.stages[0]
print(f"\nVocabulary size: {w2v_en_model.getVectors().count():,}")

%pyspark
# Evaluation
from pyspark.ml.evaluation import BinaryClassificationEvaluator

# Tokenize the validation set for prediction
preds_val_w2v_en = model_w2v_en.transform(val_en_tok)
preds_val_w2v_en.cache()

auc_eval = BinaryClassificationEvaluator(labelCol="label", rawPredictionCol="rawPrediction", metricName="areaUnderROC")
pr_eval = BinaryClassificationEvaluator(labelCol="label", rawPredictionCol="rawPrediction", metricName="areaUnderPR")

auc_w2v_en = auc_eval.evaluate(preds_val_w2v_en)
pr_auc_w2v_en = pr_eval.evaluate(preds_val_w2v_en)

tp = preds_val_w2v_en.filter((col("label") == 1.0) & (col("prediction") == 1.0)).count()
fp = preds_val_w2v_en.filter((col("label") == 0.0) & (col("prediction") == 1.0)).count()
fn = preds_val_w2v_en.filter((col("label") == 1.0) & (col("prediction") == 0.0)).count()
tn = preds_val_w2v_en.filter((col("label") == 0.0) & (col("prediction") == 0.0)).count()

p_neg = tn / (tn + fn) if (tn + fn) > 0 else 0
r_neg = tn / (tn + fp) if (tn + fp) > 0 else 0
f1_neg = 2 * p_neg * r_neg / (p_neg + r_neg) if (p_neg + r_neg) > 0 else 0

p_pos = tp / (tp + fp) if (tp + fp) > 0 else 0
r_pos = tp / (tp + fn) if (tp + fn) > 0 else 0
f1_pos = 2 * p_pos * r_pos / (p_pos + r_pos) if (p_pos + r_pos) > 0 else 0

print(f"\n===== English Word2Vec model (validation) =====")
print(f"ROC AUC:        {auc_w2v_en:.4f}")
print(f"PR AUC:         {pr_auc_w2v_en:.4f}")
print(f"Confusion matrix:")
print(f"  TN={tn:,}  FP={fp:,}")
print(f"  FN={fn:,}  TP={tp:,}")
print(f"Negative class: P={p_neg:.4f}  R={r_neg:.4f}  F1={f1_neg:.4f}")
print(f"Positive class: P={p_pos:.4f}  R={r_pos:.4f}  F1={f1_pos:.4f}")
print(f"Macro F1:       {(f1_neg + f1_pos) / 2:.4f}")

print(f"\n===== English: TF-IDF vs Word2Vec =====")
print(f"{'Metric':<20} {'TF-IDF Unigram':<18} {'TF-IDF Bigram':<18} {'Word2Vec':<18}")
print(f"{'AUC':<20} {0.9207:<18.4f} {0.9271:<18.4f} {auc_w2v_en:<18.4f}")
print(f"{'Negative F1':<20} {0.6444:<18.4f} {0.6652:<18.4f} {f1_neg:<18.4f}")
print(f"{'Macro F1':<20} {0.7929:<18.4f} {0.8058:<18.4f} {(f1_neg + f1_pos)/2:<18.4f}")
